title: Heeria melanophloeos
taxonomy: Heeria melanophloeos belongs to the family Anacardiaceae, commonly known as the cashew or poison ivy family. This classification places it within the order Sapindales and the clade rosid in the APG III system. The species Heeria melanophloeos is a member of the tribe Anacardieae. The genus Heeria is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only one species. Heeria melanophloeos is native to southern Africa.
uses: Heeria melanophloeos has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's bark and roots are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it a potential remedy for wounds and infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Heeria melanophloeos.
